author: Besting
text: REVISED 2026-06-06 (path-copy supersedes scope-addressed): install is PURE PATH-COPY — `bl install <path>` makes dest/<path> == source/<path>, siblings untouched. NO Object enum, NO merge-vs-replace logic. Path SHAPE decides: folder = MIRROR (rsync --delete; deletions propagate — this is how closes carry, dissolving the resurrection worry by addressing); file/glob = UNION (overwrite on collision, NO conflict-detection, git recovers). Per-task install (install tasks/bl-1234.md) falls out free. Bare `bl install` (no path) = config/ EXCLUDING tasks/. Never copies bin/ (gitignored, not in tree). Drop the old union-merge conflict logic entirely. spec bl-2e26 §6.

author: Unlearn
text: AMENDED by bl-8540 (merged e9b44cc): the schedule is no longer a symlink tree under config/plugins/<op>/<phase>/ — it is config/plugins.toml (§6). So 'install plugins' wiping+copying the symlink wiring (copy_wiring + the stale-symlink prune this ball adds) LARGELY DISSOLVES: the schedule is an ordinary scoped file, so 'install plugins.toml' is a plain scoped replace under the same path-copy rule; 'install config' still replaces all of config/ (incl. plugins.toml + plugin config dirs). config/plugins/ now holds only per-plugin config (config/plugins/<name>/) + gitignored bin/. KEEP unchanged: committed-tree-only (never bin/), local binary resolution + protocol validation, the subtree-only-rewrite invariant.
